definition: A cultivated perception that keeps experience from being taken as self, owned, or identified with.
notes: Use this record for the three-marks practice family when not-self is cultivated as a direct perception. In AN 10.60 the perception appears in the more relational form dukkhe anattasaññā, so this entry should stay tied to the movement from dissatisfaction toward de-appropriation rather than drifting into abstract no-self rhetoric.

example_phrases.translation: perception of not-self in what is dissatisfaction
example_phrases.notes: Canonical perception formula linking dissatisfaction and not-self.
